items.title: Mirasvit Full Page Cache Warmer Remote Code Execution (RCE) Vulnerability
items.summary: A critical PHP object injection vulnerability in Mirasvit Full Page Cache Warmer for Magento 2 allows unauthenticated attackers to achieve remote code execution by supplying a crafted serialized PHP object in the CacheWarmer cookie. This vulnerability is actively exploited and has been added to the CISA KEV catalog.

items.title: Security update available for Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source (May 2026)
items.summary: This Adobe Security Bulletin resolves critical, important, and moderate vulnerabilities in Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source, which could lead to arbitrary code execution, arbitrary file system write, application denial-of-service, and security feature bypass.

items.title: Adobe Commerce Incorrect Authorization Vulnerability
items.summary: An Incorrect Authorization vulnerability in Adobe Commerce allows a remote, unauthenticated attacker to bypass security checks and gain unauthorized write access.

items.title: Adobe Commerce Denial-of-Service (DoS) Vulnerability
items.summary: A denial-of-service vulnerability in Adobe Commerce, stemming from a vulnerable third-party component, can be triggered remotely without authentication or user interaction, causing application crashes.

items.title: PolyShell: Unrestricted file upload in Magento and Adobe Commerce
items.summary: Sansec discovered a critical flaw in Magento's REST API that allows unauthenticated attackers to upload executable files by bypassing image validation, potentially leading to remote code execution under specific web server configurations. Mass attacks exploiting this vulnerability have been observed.
